author: Augustine of Hippo
book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.

ccel.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

title: Chapter II. 1–4.

source.title: NPNF1-07. St. Augustine: Homilies on the Gospel of John; Homilies on the First Epistle of John; Soliloquies

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The miracle indeed of our Lord Jesus Christ, whereby He made the water into wine, is not marvellous to those who know that it was God’s doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who made wine on that day at the marriage feast, in those six water-pots, which He commanded to be filled with water, the self-same does this every year in vines.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even as that which the servants put into the water-pots was turned into wine by the doing of the Lord, so in like manner also is what the clouds pour forth changed into wine by the doing of the same Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But we do not wonder at the latter, because it happens every year: it has lost its marvellousness by its constant recurrence.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And yet it suggests a greater consideration than that which was done in the water-pots.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For who is there that considers the works of God, whereby this whole world is governed and regulated, who is not amazed and overwhelmed with miracles?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If he considers the vigorous power of a single grain of any seed whatever, it is a mighty thing, it inspires him with awe.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But since men, intent on a different matter, have lost the consideration of the works of God, by which they should daily praise Him as the Creator, God has, as it were, reserved to Himself the doing of certain extraordinary actions, that, by striking them with wonder, He might rouse men as from sleep to worship Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: A dead man has risen again; men marvel: so many are born daily, and none marvels.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we reflect more considerately, it is a matter of greater wonder for one to be who was not before, than for one who was to come to life again.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet the same God, the Father of our Lord Jesus Christ, doeth by His word all these things; and it is He who created that governs also.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The former miracles He did by His Word, God with Himself; the latter miracles He did by the same Word incarnate, and for us made man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: As we wonder at the things which were done by the man Jesus, so let us wonder at the things which where done by Jesus God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By Jesus God were made heaven, and earth, and the sea, all the garniture of heaven, the abounding riches of the earth, and the fruitfulness of the sea;—all these things which lie within the reach of our eyes were made by Jesus God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we look at these things, and if His own spirit is in us they in such manner please us, that we praise Him that contrived them; not in such manner that turning ourselves to the works we turn away from the Maker, and, in a manner, turning our face to the things made and our backs to Him that made them.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And these things indeed we see; they lie before our eyes.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what of those we do not see, as angels, virtues, powers, dominions, and every inhabitant of this fabric which is above the heavens, and beyond the reach of our eyes?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet angels, too, when necessary, often showed themselves to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Has not God made all these too by His Word, that is, by His only Son, our Lord Jesus Christ?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What of the human soul itself, which is not seen, and yet by its works shown in the flesh excites great admiration in those that duly reflect on them,—by whom was it made, unless by God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And through whom was it made, unless through the Son of God?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Not to speak as yet of the soul of man: the soul of any brute whatever, see how it regulates the huge body, puts forth the senses, the eyes to see, the ears to hear, the nostrils to smell, the taste to discern flavors—the members, in short, to execute their respective functions!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is it the body, not the soul, namely the inhabitant of the body, that doeth these things?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The soul is not apparent to the eyes, nevertheless it excites admiration by these its actions.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Direct now thy consideration to the soul of man, on which God has bestowed understanding to know its Creator, to discern and distinguish between good and evil, that is, between right and wrong: see how many things it does through the body!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Observe this whole world arranged in the same human commonwealth, with what administrations, with what orderly degrees of authority, with what conditions of citizenship, with what laws, manners, arts!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The whole of this is brought about by the soul, and yet this power of the soul is not visible.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When withdrawn from the body, the latter is a mere carcase: first, it in a manner preserves it from rottenness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For all flesh is corruptible, and falls off into putridity unless preserved by the soul as by a kind of seasoning.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the human soul has this quality in common with the soul of the brute; those qualities rather are to be admired which I have stated, such as belong to the mind and intellect, wherein also it is renewed after the image of its Creator, after whose image man was formed.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What will this power of the soul be when this body shall have put on incorruption, and this mortal shall have put on immortality?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If such is its power, acting through corruptible flesh, what shall be its power through a spiritual body, after the resurrection of the dead?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Yet this soul, as I have said, of admirable nature and substance, is a thing invisible, intellectual; this soul also was made by God Jesus, for He is the Word of God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “All things were made by Him, and without Him was nothing made.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When we see, therefore, such deeds wrought by Jesus God, why should we wonder at water being turned into wine by the man Jesus?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He was not made man in such manner that He lost His being God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Man was added to Him, God not lost to Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This miracle was wrought by the same who made all those things.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us not therefore wonder that God did it, but love Him because He did it in our midst, and for the purpose of our restoration.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He gives us certain intimations by the very circumstances of the case.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I suppose that it was not without cause He came to the marriage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The miracle apart, there lies something mysterious and sacramental in the very fact.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us knock, that He may open to us, and fill us with the invisible wine: for we were water, and He made us wine, made us wise; for He gave us the wisdom of His faith, whilst before we were foolish.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And it appertains, it may be, to this wisdom, together with the honor of God, and with the praise of His majesty, and with the charity of His most powerful mercy, to understand what was done in this miracle.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The Lord, on being invited, came to the marriage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What wonder if He came to that house to a marriage, having come into this world to a marriage?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, indeed, if He came not to a marriage, He has not here a bride.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But what says the apostle?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I have espoused you to one husband, to present you a chaste virgin to Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why does he fear lest the virginity of Christ’s bride should be corrupted by the subtilty of the devil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “I fear,” saith he, “lest as the serpent beguiled Eve by his subtilty, so also your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity and chastity which is in Christ.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thus has He here a bride whom He has redeemed by His blood, and to whom He has given the Holy Spirit as a pledge.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He has freed her from the bondage of the devil: He died for her sins, and is risen again for her justification.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who will make such offerings to his bride?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Men may offer to a bride every sort of earthly ornament,—gold, silver, precious stones, houses, slaves, estates, farms,—but will any give his own blood?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if one should give his own blood to his bride, he would not live to take her for his wife.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the Lord, dying without fear, gave His own blood for her, whom rising again He was to have, whom He had already united to Himself in the Virgin’s womb.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For the Word was the Bridegroom, and human flesh the bride; and both one, the Son of God, the same also being Son of man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The womb of the Virgin Mary, in which He became head of the Church, was His bridal chamber: thence He came forth, as a bridegroom from his chamber, as the Scripture foretold, “And rejoiced as a giant to run his way.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From His chamber He came forth as a bridegroom; and being invited, came to the marriage.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is because of an indubitable mystery that He appears not to acknowledge His mother, from whom as the Bridegroom He came forth, when He says to her, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What is this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Did He come to the marriage for the purpose of teaching men to treat their mothers with contempt?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Surely he to whose marriage He had come was taking a wife with the view of having children, and surely he wished to be honored by those children he would beget: had Jesus then come to the marriage in order to dishonor His mother, when marriages are celebrated and wives married with the view of having children, whom God commands to honor their parents?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Beyond all doubt, brethren, there is some mystery lurking here.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is really a matter of such importance that some,—of whom the apostle, as we have mentioned before, has forewarned us to be on our guard, saying, “I fear, lest, as the serpent beguiled Eve by his subtilty, so also your minds should be corrupted from the simplicity and chastity which is in Christ,”—taking away from the credibility of the gospel, and asserting that Jesus was not born of the Virgin Mary, used to endeavor to draw from this place an argument in support of their error, so far as to say, How could she be His mother, to whom He said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Wherefore we must answer them, and show them why the Lord said this, lest in their insanity they appear to themselves to have discovered something contrary to wholesome belief, whereby the chastity of the virgin bride may be corrupted, that is, whereby the faith of the Church may be injured.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For in very deed, brethren, their faith is corrupted who prefer a lie to the truth.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For these men, who appear to honor Christ in such wise as to deny that He had flesh, do nothing short of proclaiming Him a liar.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now they who build up a lie in men, what do they but drive the truth out of them?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They let in the devil, they drive Christ out; they let in an adulterer, shut out the bridegroom, being evidently paranymphs, or rather, the panderers of the serpent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For it is for this object they speak, that the serpent may possess, and Christ be shut out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How doth the serpent possess?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When a lie possesses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When falsehood possesses, then the serpent possesses; when truth possesses, then Christ possesses.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For Himself has said, “I am the truth;” but of that other He said, “He stood not in the truth, because the truth is not him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And Christ is the truth in such wise that thou shouldst receive the whole to be true in Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The true Word, God equal with the Father, true soul, true flesh, true man, true God, true nativity, true passion, true death, true resurrection.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If thou say that any of these is false, rottenness enters, the worms of falsehood are bred of the poison of the serpent, and nothing sound will remain.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What, then, is this, saith one, which the Lord saith, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Perhaps the Lord shows us in the sequel why He said this: “Mine hour,” saith He, “is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thus is how He saith, “Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And we must seek to know why this was said.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But first let us therefrom withstand the heretics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What says the old serpent, of old the hissing instiller of poison?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What saith he?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That Jesus had not a woman for His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence provest thou that?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: From this, saith he, because Jesus said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who has related this, that we should believe that Jesus said it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who has related it?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: None other than John the evangelist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the same John the evangelist said, “And the mother of Jesus was there.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For this is how he has told us: “The next day there was a marriage in Cana of Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And having been invited to the marriage, Jesus had come thither with His disciples.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We have here two sayings uttered by the evangelist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “The mother of Jesus was there,” said the evangelist; and it is the same evangelist that has told us what Jesus said to His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And see, brethren, how he has told us that Jesus answered His mother, having said first, “His mother said unto Him,” in order that you may keep the virginity of your heart secure against the tongue of the serpent.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Here we are told in the same Gospel, the record of the same evangelist, “The mother of Jesus was there,” and “His mother said unto Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who related this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: John the evangelist.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And what said Jesus in answer to His mother?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Who relates this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The very same Evangelist John.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: O most faithful and truth-speaking evangelist, thou tellest me that Jesus said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” why hast thou added His mother, whom He does not acknowledge?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For thou hast said that “the mother of Jesus was there,” and that “His mother said unto Him;” why didst thou not rather say, Mary was there, and Mary said unto Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Thou tellest as these two facts, “His mother said unto Him,” and “Jesus answered her, Woman, why have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why doest thou this, if it be not because both are true?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now, those men are willing to believe the evangelist in the one case, when he tells us that Jesus said to His mother, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” and yet they will not believe him in the other, when he says, “The mother of Jesus was there,” and “His mother said unto Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But who is he that resisteth the serpent and holds fast the truth, whose virginity of heart is not corrupted by the subtilty of the devil?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who believes both to be true, namely, that the mother of Jesus was there, and that Jesus made that answer to His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if he does not as yet understand in what manner Jesus said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” let him meanwhile believe that He said it, and said it, moreover, to His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let him first have the piety to believe, and he will then have fruit in understanding.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I ask you, O faithful Christians, Was the mother of Jesus there?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Answer ye, She was.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence know you?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Answer, The Gospel says it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What answer made Jesus to His mother?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Answer ye, “Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And whence know you this?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Answer, The Gospel says it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let no man corrupt this your faith, if you desire to preserve a chaste virginity for the Bridegroom.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if it be asked of you, why He made this answer to His mother, let him declare who understands; but he who does not as yet understand, let him most firmly believe that Jesus made this answer, and made it moreover to His mother.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: By this piety he will learn to understand also why Jesus answered thus, if by praying he knock at the door of truth, and do not approach it with wrangling.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Only this much, while he fancies himself to know, or is ashamed because he does not know, why Jesus answered thus, let him beware lest he be constrained to believe either that the evangelist lied when he said, “The mother of Jesus was there,” or that Jesus Himself suffered for our sins by a counterfeit death and for our justification showed counterfeit scars; and that He spoke falsely in saying, “If ye continue in my word, ye are my disciples indeed; and ye shall know the truth, and the truth shall make you free.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For if He had a false mother, false flesh, false death, false wounds in His death, false scars in His resurrection, then it will not be the truth, but rather falsehood, that shall make free those that believe on Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay, on the contrary, let falsehood yield to truth, and let all be confounded who would have themselves be accounted truth-speaking, because they endeavor to prove Christ a deceiver, and will not have it said to them, We do not believe you because you lie, when they affirm that truth itself has lied.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, if we ask them, Whence know you that Christ said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” they answer that they believe the Gospel.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Then why do they not believe the Gospel when it says, “The mother of Jesus was there,” and, “His mother said unto Him”?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Or if the Gospel lies here, how are we to believe it there, that Jesus said this, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why do not those miserable men rather faithfully believe that the Lord did so answer, not to a stranger, but to His mother; and also piously seek to know why He did so answer?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There is a great difference between him who says, I would know why Christ made this answer to His mother, and him who says, I know that it was not to His mother that Christ made this answer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is one thing to be willing to understand what is shut up, another thing to be unwilling to believe what is open.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He who says, I would know why Christ thus made answer to His mother, wishes the Gospel, in which he believes, opened up to him; but he who says, I know that it was not to His mother that Christ made this answer, accuses of falsehood the very Gospel, wherein he believed that Christ did so answer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now then, if it seem good, brethren, those men being repulsed, and ever wandering in their own blindness, unless in humility they be healed, let us inquire why our Lord answered His mother in such a manner.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He was in an extraordinary manner begotten of the Father without a mother, born of a mother without a father; without a mother He was God, without a father He was man; without a mother before all time, without a father in the end of times.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What He said was said in answer to His mother, for “the mother of Jesus was there,” and “His mother said unto Him.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All this the Gospel says.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: It is there we learn that “the mother of Jesus was there,” just where we learn that He said unto her, “Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us believe the whole; and what we do not yet understand, let us search out.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And first take care, lest perhaps, as the Manichæans found occasion for their falsehood, because the Lord said, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?” the astrologers in like manner may find occasion for their deception, in that He said, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If it was in the sense of the astrologers He said this, we have committed a sacrilege in burning their books.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But if we have acted rightly, as was done in the times of the apostles, it was not according to their notion that the Lord said, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For, say those vain-talkers and deceived seducers, thou seest that Christ was under fate, as He says, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: To whom then must we make answer first—to the heretics or to the astrologers?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For both come of the serpent, and desire to corrupt the Church’s virginity of heart, which she holds in undefiled faith.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us first reply to those whom we proposed, to whom, indeed, we have already replied in great measure.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But lest they should think that we have not what to say of the words which the Lord uttered in answer to His mother, we prepare you further against them; for I suppose what has already been said is sufficient for their refutation.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why, then, said the Son to the mother, “Woman, what have I to do with thee? mine hour is not yet come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Our Lord Jesus Christ was both God and man.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: According as He was God, He had not a mother; according as He was man, He had.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: She was the mother, then, of His flesh, of His humanity, of the weakness which for our sakes He took upon Him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the miracle which He was about to do, He was about to do according to His divine nature, not according to His weakness; according to that wherein He was God not according to that wherein He was born weak.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But the weakness of God is stronger than men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: His mother then demanded a miracle of Him; but He, about to perform divine works, so far did not recognize a human womb; saying in effect, “That in me which works a miracle was not born of thee, thou gavest not birth to my divine nature; but because my weakness was born of thee, I will recognize thee at the time when that same weakness shall hang upon the cross.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This, indeed, is the meaning of “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then it was that He recognized, who, in truth, always did know.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He knew His mother in predestination, even before He was born of her; even before, as God, He created her of whom, as man, He was to be created, He knew her as His mother: but at a certain hour in a mystery He did not recognize her; and at a certain hour which had not yet come, again in a mystery, He does recognize her.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For then did He recognize her, when that to which she gave birth was a-dying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That by which Mary was made did not die, but that which was made of Mary; not the eternity of the divine nature, but the weakness of the flesh, was dying.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He made that answer therefore, making a distinction in the faith of believers, between the who; and the how, He came.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For while He was God and the Lord of heaven and earth, He came by a mother who was a woman.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In that He was Lord of the world, Lord of heaven and earth, He was, of course, the Lord of Mary also; but in that wherein it is said, “Made of a woman, made under the law,” He was Mary’s son.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The same both the Lord of Mary and the son of Mary; the same both the Creator of Mary and created from Mary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Marvel not that He was both son and Lord.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For just as He is called the son of Mary, so likewise is He called the son of David; and son of David because son of Mary.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear the apostle openly declaring, “Who was made of the seed of David according to the flesh.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Hear Him also declared the Lord of David; let David himself declare this: “The Lord said to my Lord, Sit Thou on my right hand.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And this passage Jesus Himself brought forward to the Jews, and refuted them from it.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: How then was He both David’s son and David’s Lord?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: David’s son according to the flesh, David’s Lord according to His divinity; so also Mary’s son after the flesh, and Mary’s Lord after His majesty.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Now as she was not the mother of His divine nature, whilst it was by His divinity the miracle she asked for would be wrought, therefore He answered her, “Woman, what have I to do with thee?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But think not that I deny thee to be my mother: “Mine hour is not yet come;” for in that hour I will acknowledge thee, when the weakness of which thou art the mother comes to hang on the cross.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us prove the truth of this.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When the Lord suffered, the same evangelist tells us, who knew the mother of the Lord, and who has given us to know about her in this marriage feast,—the same, I say, tells us, “There was there near the cross the mother of Jesus; and Jesus saith to His mother, Woman, behold thy son! and to the disciple, Behold thy mother!”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He commends His mother to the care of the disciple; commends His mother, as about to die before her, and to rise again before her death.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man commends her a human being to man’s care.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: This humanity had Mary given birth to.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That hour had now come, the hour of which He had then said, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: In my opinion, brethren, we have answered the heretics.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let us now answer the astrologers.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And how do they attempt to prove that Jesus was under fate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because, say they, Himself said, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Therefore we believe Him; and if He had said, “I have no hour,” He would have excluded the astrologers: but behold, say they, He said, “Mine hour is not yet come.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If then He had said, “I have no hour,” the astrologers would have been shut out, and would have no ground for their slander; but now that He said, “Mine hour is not yet come,” how can we contradict His own words? ’Tis wonderful that the astrologers, by believing Christ’s words, endeavor to convince Christians that Christ lived under an hour of fate.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Well, let them believe Christ when He saith, “I have power to lay down my life and to take it up again: no man taketh it from me, but I lay it down of myself, and I take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Is this power then under fate?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them show us a man who has it in his power when to die, how long to live: this they can never do.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let them, therefore, believe God when He says, “I have power to lay down my life, and to take it up again;” and let them inquire why it was said, “Mine hour is not yet come;” and let them not because of these words, be imposing fate on the Maker of heaven, the Creator and Ruler of the stars.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For even if fate were from the stars, the Maker of the stars could not be subject to their destiny.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Moreover, not only Christ had not what thou callest fate, but not even hast thou, or I, or he there, or any human being whatsoever.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nevertheless, being deceived, they deceive others, and propound fallacies to men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They lay snares to catch men, and that, too, in the open streets.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: They who spread nets to catch wild beasts even do it in woods and desert places: how miserably vain are men, for catching whom the net is spread in the forum!

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When men sell themselves to men, they receive money; but these give money in order to sell themselves to vanities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For they go in to an astrologer to buy themselves masters, such as the astrologer is pleased to give them: be it Saturn, Jupiter, Mercury, or any other named profanity.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The man went in free, that having given his money he might come out a slave.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Nay, rather, had he been free he would not have gone in; but he entered whither his master Error and his mistress Avarice dragged him.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Whence also the truth says, “Every one that doeth sin is the slave of sin.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Why then did He say, “Mine hour is not yet come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Rather because, having it in His power when to die, He did not yet see it fit to use that power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Just as we, brethren, say, for example, “Now is the appointed hour for us to go out to celebrate the sacraments.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If we go out before it is necessary, do we not act perversely and absurdly?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And because we act only at the proper time, do we therefore in this action regard fate when we so express ourselves?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What means then, “Mine hour is not yet come?”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: When I know that it is the fitting time for me to suffer, when my suffering will be profitable, then I will willingly suffer.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: That hour is not yet: that thou mayest preserve both, this, “Mine hour is not yet come;” and that, “I have power to lay down my life, and power to take it again.”

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: He had come, then, having it in His power when to die.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: And surely it would not have been right were He to die before He had chosen disciples.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Had he been a man who had not his hour in his own power, he might have died before he had chosen disciples; and if haply he had died when his disciples were now chosen and instructed, it would be something conferred on him, not his own doing.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But, on the contrary, He who had come having in His power when to go, when to return, how far to advance, and for whom the regions of the grave were open, not only when dying but when rising again; He, I say, in order to show us His Church’s hope of immortality, showed in the head what it behoved the members to expect.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who has risen again in the head will also rise again in all His members.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: The hour then had not yet come, the fit time was not yet.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Disciples had to be called, the kingdom of heaven to be proclaimed, the Lord’s divinity to be shown forth in miracles, and His humanity in His very sympathy with mortal men.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: For He who hungered because He was man, fed so many thousands with five loaves because He was God; He who slept because He was man, commanded the winds and the waves because He was God.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: All these things had first to be set forth, that the evangelists might have whereof to write, that there might be what should be preached to the Church.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: But when He had done as much as He judged to be sufficient, then His hour came, not of necessity, but of will,—not of condition, but of power.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: What then, brethren?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Because we have replied to these and those, shall we say nothing as to what the water-pots signify? what the water turned into wine? what the master of the feast? what the bridegroom? what in mystery the mother of Jesus? what the marriage itself?

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: We must speak of all these, but we must not burden you.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: I would have preached to you in Christ’s name yesterday also, when the usual sermon was due to you, my beloved, but I was hindered by certain necessities.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: If you please then, holy brethren, let us defer until to-morrow what pertains to the hidden meaning of this translation, and not burden both your and our own weakness.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: There are many of you, perhaps, who have to-day come together on account of the solemnity of the day, not to hear the sermon.

verses.book: Lectures or Tractates on the Gospel According to St. John.
verses.text: Let those who come to-morrow come to hear, so that we may not defraud those who are eager to learn, nor burden those who are fastidious.
